task: I’m trying to set up a simple maintenance routine for a couple of things around the house so they don’t turn into bigger problems later. Can you help me sketch out what to check, how often, and what’s worth replacing before it fails?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — the simplest way is to build a **basic preventive maintenance loop**:

## 1) Check the right things on a schedule
For each item, track:
- **visible condition**
- **performance**
- **noise / vibration / smell**
- **leaks / looseness / damage**
- **consumables** like filters, batteries, belts, batteries, seals

## 2) Use three intervals
- **Monthly**: quick visual and functional check
- **Seasonally**: deeper check before heavy-use periods
- **Annually**: service, clean, replace wear items

## 3) Replace before failure when the item is:
- visibly worn, cracked, dirty, or corroded
- past its normal service life
- causing reduced performance
- making new noise or heat
- repeatedly needing attention

## 4) Keep a tiny log
Just note:
- date
- what you checked
- anything abnormal
- what you replaced
- when to check again

## Example for common household items
### HVAC/furnace
- Monthly: filter, vents, odd noises, error codes
- Seasonally: thermostat, blower airflow, condensate drain
- Replace before failure: filter, batteries, flame sensor if recurring issues, worn belts if present

### Smoke/CO alarms
- Monthly: test button
- Annually: replace batteries if needed
- Replace before failure: any alarm older than its rated life, or one that chirps repeatedly

### Water heater
- Monthly: leaks, rust, pressure relief discharge
- Annually: flush sediment if applicable
- Replace before failure: corroded fittings, failing anode, repeated pilot/ignition issues

### Sump pump
- Monthly: test cycle
- Seasonally: clean pit, check float
- Replace before failure: sticking float, weak discharge, noisy motor

### Fridge/freezer
- Monthly: door seals, temps, coil dust
- Seasonally: clean condenser coils
- Replace before failure: cracked gasket, weak cooling, noisy fan/compressor

If you want, I can turn this into a **simple home maintenance checklist by item** with intervals and “replace when…” triggers.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, the structure is fine, but it’s still pretty generic. I want something I can actually use without guessing.

Give me a tighter checklist by item — like:
- what to inspect
- how often
- what failure signs matter
- what parts are worth replacing proactively
- what can just be watched

And if you can, split it into **high-risk stuff** first — water, heat, electrical, then the rest. I don’t want a laundry list of obvious nonsense.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, **high-risk-first** home maintenance checklist you can actually use.

## 1) Water risk first
### Water heater
**Check:**
- leaks at base, valves, fittings
- rust/corrosion
- unusual noise
- hot water performance
- temperature/pressure relief discharge

**How often:**
- monthly: visual leak check
- yearly: flush sediment if applicable
- every 1–2 years: deeper inspection

**Watch:**
- small sediment noise
- slight temp fluctuation
- minor condensation that’s clearly normal

**Replace proactively:**
- anode rod if applicable and accessible
- relief valve if it’s old, sticking, or has discharged
- corroded flexible connectors

**Stop / call for help:**
- active leak
- rust at tank seam
- pressure relief valve discharging repeatedly
- gas smell or combustion issue on gas units

---

### Sump pump
**Check:**
- float movement
- pump starts/stops properly
- discharge line clear
- backup power if present

**How often:**
- monthly: test cycle
- before heavy rain seasons: full check

**Watch:**
- occasional longer run time
- slight vibration
- dirty pit that still drains

**Replace proactively:**
- float switch if sticky or intermittent
- check valve if worn/noisy
- battery backup if weak

**Stop / call for help:**
- pump doesn’t start
- runs but doesn’t move water
- repeated cycling
- burning smell or electrical issue

---

### Under-sink / appliance supply lines
**Check:**
- drips
- swollen hoses
- corrosion at fittings
- shutoff valve operation

**How often:**
- monthly quick look
- yearly touch/test shutoff valves

**Watch:**
- slight mineral staining with no active leak

**Replace proactively:**
- braided supply hoses older than about 5–10 years
- cracked or bulging hoses
- stiff or leaking shutoff valves

**Stop / act now:**
- any active drip
- hose bulge
- valve won’t close

---

## 2) Heat / fire risk
### Furnace / HVAC
**Check:**
- filter
- vents/returns
- blower noise/airflow
- error codes
- ignition behavior
- condensate drain if high-efficiency

**How often:**
- monthly in heating/cooling season
- seasonally before heavy use

**Watch:**
- one-off startup thump
- slightly longer run time
- mild dust smell at first start of season

**Replace proactively:**
- air filter
- thermostat batteries if used
- flame sensor if recurring ignition dropout
- worn belts if your unit has them

**Stop / call for help:**
- gas smell
- repeated ignition failure
- burning smell
- limit trips / short cycling
- weak airflow with burners on

---

### Smoke / CO alarms
**Check:**
- test button
- chirps
- expiry date
- mounting location

**How often:**
- monthly test
- yearly battery check/replacement if needed

**Watch:**
- occasional false chirp if battery-related and quickly resolved

**Replace proactively:**
- alarms at end of life
- batteries per manufacturer schedule
- the whole unit if it’s old or unreliable

**Stop / act now:**
- any real alarm
- repeated unexplained alarms
- CO alarm activation = treat seriously immediately

---

## 3) Electrical risk
### Main outlets / cords / power strips
**Check:**
- heat
- discoloration
- loose plugs
- cracked cords
- buzzing or sparking

**How often:**
- monthly visual check in high-use areas
- after any overload event

**Watch:**
- slight cosmetic wear with no heat or odor

**Replace proactively:**
- damaged cords
- loose power strips
- old surge protectors if they’ve taken hits or are visibly worn

**Stop / act now:**
- warmth at outlet
- burning smell
- sparking
- repeated breaker trips
- loose or wobbly receptacle

---

### Breaker panel
**Check:**
- unusual heat
- buzzing
- corrosion
- water intrusion
- tripped breakers

**How often:**
- yearly visual check
- after storms or leaks

**Watch:**
- one isolated trip from a known overload

**Replace proactively:**
- nothing casually here; panel work is usually “fix what’s wrong,” not routine swap

**Stop / call for help:**
- burning smell
- hot panel cover
- repeated trips
- rust/water
- buzzing/arcing sounds

---

## 4) Mechanical wear items
### Fridge / freezer
**Check:**
- door seal
- coil dust
- temperature stability
- fan noise

**How often:**
- monthly seals and temps
- seasonally clean coils

**Watch:**
- normal compressor cycling
- minor gasket wear if sealing okay

**Replace proactively:**
- cracked door gasket
- weak fan motor if noisy/intermittent

**Stop / investigate:**
- food warming
- compressor running constantly
- loud new grinding/clicking

---

### Garage door opener / door hardware
**Check:**
- balance
- safety reverse
- rollers/hinges
- cable condition

**How often:**
- monthly safety reverse test
- seasonally hardware check

**Watch:**
- light roller noise

**Replace proactively:**
- worn rollers
- frayed cables
- weak springs only by a pro

**Stop / call for help:**
- door binding
- cable damage
- failed safety reverse
- loud snapping/banging

---

## Simple rule for “replace vs watch”
### Replace proactively if:
- it’s a cheap wear item
- failure would cause water, heat, or safety risk
- it’s visibly worn, cracked, corroded, or intermittent
- it’s past its normal life and easy to swap

### Just watch if:
- it’s stable
- performance is normal
- the issue is cosmetic or one-off
- there’s no safety or damage risk

---

## Easy household schedule
### Monthly
- Water heater leak check
- Sump pump test
- HVAC filter / vents / noises
- Smoke/CO alarm test
- Quick outlet/cord visual check

### Seasonally
- HVAC deeper check
- Clean fridge coils
- Check door seals
- Test garage door safety reverse
- Look for leaks under sinks and around appliances

### Yearly
- Replace any expired alarms
- Flush water heater if applicable
- Review hoses, cords, seals, and valves
- Have any gas or electrical concerns inspected

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** with boxes you can reuse each month.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little broad in spots though.

What I’d want next is the same thing, but in a cleaner table and with a hard line between:
- **must replace now / soon**
- **monitor only**
- **call a pro**

Also, don’t bury the useful stuff in generic language. For example, on hoses, valves, alarms, filters, batteries — I want actual replacement triggers, not just “if worn.” Same for electrical: what’s normal wear vs what means the thing is junk.

If you can, make it look like something I could print and stick in a notebook: item, interval, what to look for, action. Keep the fluff out.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, printable version with a hard split between:

- **Replace now / soon**
- **Monitor only**
- **Call a pro**

## Home maintenance quick table

| Item | Check interval | What to look for | Action |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Smoke / CO alarms** | Monthly test, yearly age check | No test response, chirping that keeps coming back, alarm past rated life (often 7–10 years), yellowing/visible damage | **Replace now** if expired or unreliable; **call a pro** if CO alarm sounds or keeps going off |
| **Water heater** | Monthly visual, yearly service | Leak at base, rust at tank seam, relief valve dripping repeatedly, loud popping/rumbling, inconsistent hot water | **Call a pro** for leaks, rust, or relief valve issues; **replace soon** if tank is old and showing corrosion |
| **Supply hoses** (washer, sink, fridge, dishwasher) | Monthly visual, yearly touch check | Bulges, cracks, kinks, corrosion at fittings, dampness, stiff braided hose, plastic hose older than ~5 years | **Replace now** if bulged/cracked/leaking; **replace soon** if old plastic or any corrosion at ends |
| **Shutoff valves** | Yearly | Won’t turn, drips when operated, seeping at stem, frozen/corroded handle | **Replace now** if leaking or won’t close; **call a pro** if valve is seized or behind a wall |
| **Furnace / HVAC filter** | Monthly in use | Dirty enough that light doesn’t pass easily, visibly loaded with dust, reduced airflow | **Replace now** if dirty; don’t “monitor” a clogged filter |
| **Furnace / HVAC unit** | Monthly in season | Burning smell, repeated ignition failure, short cycling, weak airflow, limit trips, error codes | **Call a pro** for gas smell, repeated faults, or limit trips; **replace/filter** only if it’s just the filter |
| **Fridge door gasket** | Monthly | Cracks, tears, hard spots, door not sealing, condensation around door edge | **Replace soon** if seal is poor; **monitor** if cosmetic only and seal is good |
| **Fridge condenser coils** | Seasonally | Heavy dust/lint buildup, compressor running constantly, poor cooling | **Clean now**; **call a pro** if cleaning doesn’t fix cooling |
| **Sump pump** | Monthly test, before rainy season | Doesn’t start, runs but no water moves, float sticks, repeated cycling, loud grinding | **Replace soon** if float/check valve is failing; **call a pro** if it won’t pump or smells electrical |
| **Garage door safety reverse** | Monthly | Door doesn’t reverse on obstruction, jerky movement, frayed cable, broken spring | **Call a pro** for cable/spring issues; **stop using** if safety reverse fails |
| **Power cords / plugs** | Monthly visual | Cracked insulation, heat, discoloration, loose plug fit, sparking, exposed wire | **Replace now** if damaged or hot; **call a pro** if outlet is loose or arcing |
| **Outlets / power strips** | Monthly in high-use areas | Warmth, buzzing, scorch marks, loose receptacle, repeated breaker trips | **Call a pro** if hot/buzzing/scorched; **replace now** power strip if damaged |
| **Breaker panel** | Yearly or after storms/leaks | Rust, water intrusion, buzzing, burning smell, hot cover, frequent trips | **Call a pro now** — don’t treat panel issues as routine DIY |

---

## Hard rules by action

### Replace now / soon
Use this when you see:
- active leak or seep
- crack, bulge, fray, scorch, rust, or corrosion
- repeated failure or intermittent operation
- expired alarm or dead test response
- filter visibly loaded
- hose older than its useful life and easy to swap

### Monitor only
Use this when:
- the item is working normally
- wear is cosmetic only
- the issue happened once and did not repeat
- there’s no heat, leak, smell, noise, or safety concern

### Call a pro
Use this when you have:
- gas smell
- CO alarm
- electrical burning smell, sparking, buzzing, or hot panel/outlet
- repeated breaker trips
- furnace ignition problems or limit trips
- water heater tank rust/leak
- garage door spring/cable issues
- sump pump failure during water risk

---

## Specific replacement triggers

### Hoses
Replace if:
- any bulge, crack, or damp fitting
- plastic supply hose older than about **5 years**
- braided hose older than about **10 years** or showing corrosion/wear
- washer hose has ever burst or is visibly degraded

### Batteries
Replace if:
- smoke/CO alarm chirps after a fresh battery
- battery is at the manufacturer’s replacement interval
- device becomes unreliable on battery
- battery compartment is corroded

### Smoke / CO alarms
Replace the whole unit if:
- it’s past the printed end-of-life date
- it fails the test button
- it gives repeated false alarms after battery replacement
- it’s yellowed, damaged, or missing the date label

### Filters
Replace if:
- visibly dirty
- airflow drops
- furnace runs longer than usual
- filter is wet, collapsed, or warped

### Valves
Replace if:
- stem leaks when turned
- handle is seized
- valve won’t fully close
- corrosion is present at the body or stem

---

## Simple notebook format
You can print this and fill it in:

**Item:**  
**Location:**  
**Last checked:**  
**Condition:**  
**Action:** Replace now / Monitor / Call pro  
**Next check:**  

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checklist with checkboxes** and remove even more wording so it’s ready to print.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, yeah. But it still needs a couple things before it’s actually usable.

Two fixes:
1. make the actions more concrete — not just “replace soon” / “call a pro,” but what exactly crosses the line
2. split the table by system, with the high-risk stuff first and the rest after. I don’t want to hunt through a mixed list.

Also, for electrical, I’d want a clearer distinction between:
- normal wear: dusty, old-looking, but stable
- junk: heat, looseness, arcing, discoloration, repeat trips, cracked insulation, that kind of thing

And on alarms/hoses/valves/filters/batteries, I’m still missing the exact triggers. Example: a smoke alarm that chirps once because of low battery is one thing. One that chirps after a new battery, or fails the test, that’s junk. That’s the kind of line I mean.

So yeah — same format, cleaner, but tighten the decision points. Notebook-useful, not generic.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the tighter, notebook-style version with **harder decision points** and **systems split by risk**.

---

# 1) Water risk

| Item | Check interval | What to look for | Action |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Water heater** | Monthly | Water at base, rust at tank seam, relief valve dripping more than a brief test discharge, popping/rumbling that’s new, hot water suddenly worse | **Replace/repair now** if tank seam is rusty or leaking; **call a pro** for relief valve discharge, gas smell, or unstable combustion |
| **Supply hoses** (sink, toilet, washer, fridge, dishwasher) | Monthly visual, yearly touch | Bulge, crack, kink, damp fitting, corrosion at ends, hose rubs against metal, hose older than service life | **Replace now** if any bulge/crack/dampness; **replace soon** if plastic hose is old or braided hose has corrosion/wear at the ends |
| **Shutoff valves** | Yearly | Won’t turn, drips at stem, seeping after use, handle loose, valve won’t fully close | **Replace now** if it leaks or won’t shut off; **call a pro** if seized, behind wall, or tied to a bigger plumbing issue |
| **Sump pump** | Monthly test, before rainy season | Doesn’t start, runs but no water moves, float sticks, repeated cycling, grinding, burning smell | **Call a pro now** if it won’t pump or smells electrical; **replace soon** if float/check valve is intermittent or noisy |

### Water hard lines
- **Replace now:** active leak, bulge, crack, rust-through, damp fitting, valve won’t close
- **Monitor only:** dry, stable, no change in function, no corrosion progression
- **Call a pro:** tank seam rust, electrical smell on pump, hidden valve, repeated sump failure

---

# 2) Heat / fire risk

| Item | Check interval | What to look for | Action |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Smoke / CO alarms** | Monthly test, yearly age check | No test response, chirps after a fresh battery, repeated false alarms, yellowing, missing date label, expired life | **Replace now** if test fails or expired; **call a pro / emergency** if CO alarm sounds or keeps sounding |
| **Furnace / HVAC filter** | Monthly in use | Filter is visibly loaded, collapsed, wet, warped, or airflow drops | **Replace now** if dirty or damaged — don’t “monitor” a clogged filter |
| **Furnace / HVAC unit** | Monthly in season | Gas smell, repeated ignition failure, short cycling, weak airflow, limit trips, new burning smell, error code repeat | **Call a pro now** for gas smell, repeated faults, limit trips, or rollout-like symptoms |
| **Thermostat batteries** | Yearly or when low-battery warning appears | Low battery warning, blank display, unreliable calls for heat/cool | **Replace now** if low or unstable; monitor only if stable |

### Furnace-specific trigger lines
- **Replace/filter now:** dirty filter, weak airflow caused by filter restriction
- **Call a pro:** gas smell, flame dropout that repeats, limit trips, burner rollout, repeated lockout
- **Monitor only:** one-off startup thump, brief dust smell at first seasonal start, one isolated missed call that clears

---

# 3) Electrical risk

| Item | Check interval | What to look for | Action |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Power cords / plugs** | Monthly visual | Cracked insulation, exposed wire, scorch marks, melted plug, loose blades, heat, sparking, intermittent power when moved | **Replace now** if cracked, hot, or discolored; **call a pro** if the outlet itself is loose or arcing |
| **Outlets / receptacles** | Monthly in high-use areas | Warmth, buzzing, scorch marks, loose plug fit, flicker, repeated breaker trips | **Call a pro now** if hot, buzzing, scorched, or tripping repeatedly |
| **Power strips / surge protectors** | Monthly | Cracks, heat, failed switch, loose outlets, damaged cord, repeated overload trips | **Replace now** if damaged or overloaded; **do not keep using** a hot or cracked strip |
| **Breaker panel** | Yearly or after storms/leaks | Rust, water intrusion, buzzing, burning smell, hot cover, frequent trips | **Call a pro now** — panel issues are not routine DIY |

### Electrical hard line
**Normal wear:** dusty, old-looking, but cool, stable, and not loose  
**Junk / stop using:** heat, looseness, arcing, scorch marks, cracked insulation, buzzing, repeat trips, melted plastic

---

# 4) Mechanical / appliance wear

| Item | Check interval | What to look for | Action |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Fridge door gasket** | Monthly | Cracks, tears, hard spots, door doesn’t seal, condensation around edge | **Replace soon** if seal is poor; **monitor only** if cosmetic and still sealing |
| **Fridge condenser coils** | Seasonally | Heavy dust/lint, compressor runs constantly, poor cooling | **Clean now**; **call a pro** if cooling doesn’t recover |
| **Garage door safety reverse** | Monthly | Door doesn’t reverse on obstruction, jerky movement, frayed cable, damaged track, broken spring | **Stop using / call a pro** if reverse fails or cable/spring is damaged |
| **Garage door rollers/hinges** | Seasonally | Grinding, flat spots, wobble, visible wear | **Replace soon** if noisy or binding; **monitor** if light noise only and movement is smooth |

---

# 5) Exact replacement triggers

## Smoke / CO alarms
**Replace the whole unit now if:**
- it fails the test button
- it chirps after a fresh battery
- it’s past the printed end-of-life date
- it’s yellowed, damaged, or missing the date label
- it gives repeated false alarms after battery replacement

**Monitor only if:**
- one low-battery chirp stops after battery replacement
- it tests normally and is within age limit

---

## Hoses
**Replace now if:**
- any bulge, crack, split, or dampness at the fitting
- hose is kinked and stays kinked
- hose shows corrosion at the ends
- washer hose has any visible degradation
- plastic supply hose is old enough that you don’t trust it anymore

**Replace soon if:**
- braided hose has surface wear or corrosion but no leak yet
- hose is older than its useful life and easy to swap during routine work

**Monitor only if:**
- dry, flexible, no corrosion, no deformation, no leak

---

## Valves
**Replace now if:**
- valve leaks when opened or closed
- stem seeping is visible
- handle is seized or broken
- valve won’t fully shut off

**Call a pro if:**
- valve is behind a wall
- shutting it off affects a larger system
- it’s part of a gas line or major supply line

---

## Filters
**Replace now if:**
- visibly loaded with dust
- airflow drops
- filter is wet, warped, collapsed, or sucked inward

**Monitor only if:**
- still clean enough to pass air normally and no performance change

---

## Batteries
**Replace now if:**
- low-battery warning appears
- device becomes unreliable
- battery compartment is corroded
- alarm chirps after a fresh battery

**Monitor only if:**
- device is stable, no warning, and battery is within normal service window

---

# 6) Simple notebook format

Use this for each item:

**Item:**  
**Location:**  
**Last checked:**  
**Condition:**  
**Trigger met?** Replace now / Replace soon / Monitor / Call pro  
**Next check:**  

---

If you want, I can compress this into a **single-page printable checklist** with just the table and the action labels, no extra explanation.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but not there yet. Still got some mush in the edges.

What I need is the actual decision line in the table itself, not buried below it. Like:
- low-battery chirp once = replace battery
- chirp after fresh battery = replace alarm
- test button fails = junk
- outlet warm to the touch = stop using / call electrician
- outlet just dusty/old-looking = monitor

Same idea for hoses and valves. I don’t want “use your judgment” language anywhere near it. I want the trigger written out right there.

Also the split by system is good, but I’d make it even cleaner:
1. water
2. heat/fire
3. electrical
4. appliances / mechanical

And under each item, keep it to four lines max:
- inspect
- interval
- replace if
- otherwise

If you want to make this actually notebook-usable, strip the extra headings and the generic hard-line stuff. Put the thresholds in the row. That's the part I can use without second-guessing it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the **tight notebook version** with the trigger written in the row and the systems split exactly as you asked.

---

# 1) Water

| Item | Inspect | Interval | Replace if | Otherwise |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Supply hoses** (sink, toilet, washer, fridge, dishwasher) | Bulge, crack, kink that stays, damp fitting, corrosion at ends | Monthly visual; yearly touch | **Any bulge/crack/dampness = replace now**; **plastic hose older than ~5 years = replace soon**; **braided hose with end corrosion/wear = replace soon** | Monitor if dry, flexible, no corrosion, no deformation |
| **Shutoff valves** | Stem seep, drip, handle stiff/broken, won’t fully close | Yearly | **Any leak or won’t shut off = replace now**; **seized valve = replace now**; **behind-wall valve that misbehaves = call a pro** | Monitor if dry, turns fully, and closes cleanly |
| **Water heater** | Leak at base, rust at tank seam, relief valve dripping repeatedly, new rumbling/popping | Monthly visual; yearly service | **Tank seam rust or active leak = replace now**; **relief valve repeatedly discharging = call a pro**; **rusted fittings/corrosion = replace soon** | Monitor if dry, normal heat, no new noise |
| **Sump pump** | Won’t start, runs but no water moves, float sticks, repeated cycling, grinding, burning smell | Monthly test; before rainy season | **Won’t pump = call a pro now**; **burning smell/electrical smell = call a pro now**; **sticky float or noisy check valve = replace soon** | Monitor if starts/stops cleanly and discharge is clear |

---

# 2) Heat / Fire

| Item | Inspect | Interval | Replace if | Otherwise |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Smoke / CO alarms** | Test button, chirps, age label, yellowing, false alarms | Monthly test; yearly age check | **Test fails = replace now**; **chirp after fresh battery = replace alarm**; **past end-of-life date = replace now** | Monitor if test passes and no warnings |
| **Furnace / HVAC filter** | Dust load, collapse, wetness, warping, airflow drop | Monthly in use | **Visibly dirty, wet, collapsed, or warped = replace now** | Monitor only if clean and airflow is normal |
| **Furnace / HVAC unit** | Gas smell, repeated ignition failure, short cycling, weak airflow, limit trips, new burning smell, repeat error code | Monthly in season | **Gas smell = stop now / call pro**; **limit trip or repeated ignition failure = call pro**; **burning smell = stop now / call pro** | Monitor if one-off startup thump or brief dust smell only |
| **Thermostat batteries** | Low-battery warning, blank display, unstable control | Yearly or when warned | **Low-battery warning = replace now**; **blank or unstable display = replace now** | Monitor if stable and no warning |

---

# 3) Electrical

| Item | Inspect | Interval | Replace if | Otherwise |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Power cords / plugs** | Cracks, exposed wire, scorch marks, melted plug, loose blades, heat, sparking | Monthly visual | **Any crack, heat, scorch, melt, or exposed wire = replace now**; **sparking = stop using now** | Monitor if dusty and stable |
| **Outlets / receptacles** | Warmth, buzzing, scorch marks, loose plug fit, flicker, repeated breaker trips | Monthly in high-use areas | **Warm to the touch = stop using / call electrician**; **buzzing, scorch, looseness, or repeat trips = call electrician** | Monitor if just dusty or old-looking but cool and tight |
| **Power strips / surge protectors** | Cracks, heat, failed switch, loose outlets, damaged cord, overload trips | Monthly | **Hot, cracked, or damaged = replace now**; **repeated overload trips = replace now** | Monitor if cool, stable, and undamaged |
| **Breaker panel** | Rust, water intrusion, buzzing, burning smell, hot cover, frequent trips | Yearly or after storms/leaks | **Any burning smell, heat, buzzing, or water = call electrician now**; **repeat trips = call electrician** | Monitor only if dry, cool, and quiet |

---

# 4) Appliances / Mechanical

| Item | Inspect | Interval | Replace if | Otherwise |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Fridge door gasket** | Cracks, tears, hard spots, condensation at edge, door not sealing | Monthly | **Crack/tear or poor seal = replace soon** | Monitor if cosmetic only and seal is good |
| **Fridge condenser coils** | Dust/lint buildup, compressor running constantly, poor cooling | Seasonally | **Heavy dust + poor cooling = clean now**; **if cooling stays poor after cleaning = call pro** | Monitor if clean and temps are stable |
| **Garage door safety reverse** | Door fails to reverse on obstruction, jerky movement, frayed cable, broken spring | Monthly | **Reverse fails = stop using / call pro**; **frayed cable or broken spring = call pro** | Monitor if reverse works and movement is smooth |
| **Garage door rollers/hinges** | Grinding, wobble, flat spots, visible wear | Seasonally | **Grinding or binding = replace soon** | Monitor if light noise only and movement is smooth |

---

## Exact trigger lines
- **Smoke alarm chirps once, then stops after battery change = replace battery**
- **Smoke alarm chirps after fresh battery = replace alarm**
- **Smoke alarm test fails = replace alarm**
- **Outlet warm to the touch = stop using / call electrician**
- **Outlet dusty/old-looking but cool and tight = monitor**
- **Hose bulge, crack, or damp fitting = replace now**
- **Plastic hose older than ~5 years = replace soon**
- **Braided hose with end corrosion = replace soon**
- **Valve leaks or won’t shut off = replace now**
- **Valve seized = replace now**
- **Power strip hot or cracked = replace now**

If you want, I can turn this into a **single-page checklist with checkboxes only** and no extra explanation.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s usable.

The rows are finally doing the work instead of the notes underneath. Exact trigger lines in the table itself — that’s what I wanted.

A couple of those are still borderline in real life, but for a notebook checklist this is fine. The water / heat / electrical split is clean. Electrical line is especially better: cool and tight = watch, warm / loose / arcing / scorched = junk. That’s the kind of distinction I mean.

If you want to do one more pass, I’d only tighten the appliance side a little more. But this version is already printable.
